title: Frontend is a design-system showcase only — no functional application screens exist
body:
## Issue description

The only client page currently deployed is `client/src/pages/Home/Home.tsx`. Its content is entirely a design-system demonstration: button size variants, typography scale samples, semantic colour-token swatches, and a theme switcher. No actual application functionality exists in the frontend.

The navigation links in the header (`href="#groups"` and `href="#events"`) are plain anchor links that scroll nowhere. The "Get started" and "Learn more" hero buttons have no `onClick` handlers and perform no action. The page is, in effect, a static placeholder.

This violates the product requirements documented in `.github/copilot-instructions.md`, which describe a fully functional group calendar application with user profile management, group management, event management, event capture, event chat, event checklist, and event location features.

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

The frontend must be replaced with or evolved into a real application. At minimum the following screens must exist:

- **Login / authentication landing** — redirects unauthenticated users to Entra ID (see related auth-gate issue).
- **Groups list** — shows the authenticated user's groups; allows creating a new group.
- **Group detail** — shows the group's event list (only events the user is explicitly invited to or created); allows managing members if the user is an owner/admin.
- **Event detail** — shows event information, RSVP controls, and navigation to event-scoped collaboration features (chat, checklist, location).
- **Event creation / event capture** — form or capture flow (text/voice/image) for creating a new event.
- **User profile** — view and edit own profile.

The component showcase and token swatches belong in a Storybook or a dedicated design-system playground, not in the production app. They must be removed from production-facing routes.

## Additional notes

- All backend modules are fully implemented with API endpoints: identity-profile, group-management, group-membership, event-management, event-capture, event-chat, event-checklist, event-location. The frontend needs only to call these existing APIs.
- The group-level event list must apply the server-enforced filter (only events the requesting user is creator of or explicitly invited to). The client must not attempt to display or hint at events the user cannot access. See `docs/architecture/access-control.md`.
- Event-scoped collaboration features (chat, checklist, location) must be accessible only from within an event detail screen — never surfaced at group level.
- The design system (`client/src/design-system/`) and shared components (`client/src/components/`) are complete and production-ready; use them directly.
- Client-side routing must be added (React Router or equivalent — see related routing issue) before multi-page navigation is possible.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A login/auth landing screen exists and is the only thing rendered for unauthenticated users.
- [ ] An authenticated groups-list screen shows the user's groups and allows group creation.
- [ ] A group-detail screen shows the event list filtered to the user's accessible events only.
- [ ] An event-detail screen shows event information and RSVP controls.
- [ ] Navigation between screens works via client-side routing (no full-page reloads).
- [ ] The component showcase and colour-token swatches are removed from production-facing routes.
- [ ] The hero "Get started" and "Learn more" buttons are replaced with functional navigation actions.
- [ ] All new screens use only design-system tokens (no hard-coded colours).
- [ ] API calls include the Bearer token acquired from MSAL.
- [ ] Tests cover each screen's render and basic interaction paths.
- [ ] CI passes.
